highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.183 Released: Safer Auto Mode for Destructive Git Commands
highlights.summary: The latest release blocks dangerous git commands like `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, and `git stash drop` when you didn't explicitly ask to discard local work. It also prevents `git commit --amend` on commits the agent didn't make in the current session, protecting your work history. This is a significant safety improvement for developers who let Claude Code operate in auto mode.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.181: Set Config Values Directly from the Prompt
highlights.summary: You can now use `/config key=value` syntax (e.g. `/config thinking=false`) to change settings on the fly without editing config files, and it works in interactive mode, `-p` flag, and Remote Control. This release also adds `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` for macOS users who need sandboxed commands to send Apple Events. These quality-of-life improvements make it much faster to tweak Claude Code's behavior mid-session.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.178: Fine-Grained Permission Rules with Parameter Matching
highlights.summary: A new `Tool(param:value)` syntax lets you write permission rules that match specific tool input parameters, including wildcard `*` support — for example, `Agent(model:opus)` can block Opus-powered subagents from running. Nested `.claude/skills` directories now also load automatically when you're working in those subdirectories. These features give power users much tighter control over what Claude Code is allowed to do in complex projects.

highlights.title: Anthropic Pauses Subscription Credit Change for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic had announced that Claude Code's `-p` flag and Agent SDK usage would move from subscription rate limits to a separate monthly credit pool starting in June, but has now paused that change after user feedback. If you rely on Claude Code's `-p` mode for automation scripts, your current subscription limits remain in effect for now. Keep an eye on official communications as Anthropic works out the new billing model.

highlights.title: Community Build: Claude Code Integration for Visual Studio (Native Diff Accept/Reject)
highlights.summary: A developer built a Visual Studio (not VS Code) extension that brings Claude Code's native diff accept/reject workflow to the IDE, using the same protocol as the official VS Code and JetBrains plugins. If you're on a team that uses Visual Studio for .NET or C++ work, this fills a real gap that Anthropic hasn't addressed officially yet. Check the GitHub repo's GIF demo to see the diff UI in action before installing.

highlights.title: 60-Minute Complete Claude Code Masterclass (Bilibili) — From Installation to Advanced Usage
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Chinese-language Bilibili tutorial covers everything from initial setup to advanced Claude Code techniques in a single 60-minute video, accompanied by full documentation. It's especially useful for developers in China looking for a direct-access guide with beginner-to-advanced progression. The creator spent three weeks preparing the material, making it one of the most thorough single-video resources available.

highlights.title: Claude Code Full Tutorial for Beginners (YouTube)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through Claude Code from scratch, covering setup, core workflows, and practical usage patterns inside VS Code. It's a good starting point if you prefer video learning and want a guided walkthrough before diving into the official docs. Pair it with the latest release notes to make sure the features shown match the current version.

highlights.title: New Developer Builds Production Bookkeeping App Using Claude Code
highlights.summary: A self-described non-senior developer shipped QuarterPerfect, a real UK bookkeeping tool for MTD ITSA compliance, using AI-assisted coding with Claude Code over about a month. The Hacker News post details the workflow, challenges, and lessons learned — a practical example of what beginners can realistically accomplish today. It's an encouraging read if you're wondering whether Claude Code can help you ship real products without deep prior experience.

tips_of_the_day: Before starting any session that involves refactoring or branch cleanup, explicitly tell Claude Code 'do not discard any local changes' — even with v2.1.183's new guards, stating your intent clearly upfront gives the agent better context and prevents misunderstandings.

Use the new `/config thinking=false` slash command to quickly disable extended thinking when you're doing rapid iterative edits where speed matters more than deep reasoning, then re-enable it with `/config thinking=true` for architecture-level decisions in the same session.

If you're managing a monorepo, take advantage of nested `.claude/skills` directories (added in v2.1.178) to store package-specific prompt snippets and instructions right alongside the code they relate to — this keeps your root CLAUDE.md clean and makes context automatically available when Claude Code navigates into a subdirectory.
